message:
feat(undo): Cx-first UndoExecutor::execute (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Expand the Cx-first surface into undo.rs. `UndoExecutor::execute`
is the public dispatch entry point that drives three storage
queries before dispatching to strategy-specific handlers
(`execute_workflow_abort` / `execute_pane_close`). Without cx
threading, a long-running undo dispatch ignores caller
cancellation.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `UndoExecutor::execute_with_cx(cx, request)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `execute`. Checkpoints at 3 seams before the
    strategy dispatch:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns before the
        `get_action_history` query.
      - between get_action_history and get_action_undo: a cancel
        here skips the second storage read when the result would
        be discarded.
      - before strategy dispatch: a cancel here surfaces with the
        strategy name embedded in the error message so the caller
        can see which undo path was about to run.

Cancellation-safety contract: all 3 checkpoints fire BEFORE any
mutating operation (wezterm kill_pane, mark_undone, etc.). A
cancel during any checkpoint window leaves no side effects on
storage or wezterm state. The strategy handlers themselves remain
on the ambient path — they use `self.wezterm.*` (Cx-first since
tick 46 but not threaded here yet) and `self.mark_undone` (writes
to `action_undo` table, no _with_cx surface). Follow-on: thread
cx into the strategy handlers to cover the full dispatch depth.

Regression tests (2 new, parity coverage):

  * `execute_with_cx_matches_legacy_on_not_found` — exercises
    the path where `get_action_history` returns empty
    (action_id=99999, no such action). Both variants return
    `NotApplicable` with a "not found" message. Asserts
    UndoExecutionResult parity across outcome, action_id,
    strategy, message, guidance.

  * `execute_with_cx_matches_legacy_on_no_metadata` — exercises
    the path where `get_action_history` succeeds but
    `get_action_undo` returns None (action exists, no undo
    metadata recorded). This specifically proves the SECOND
    storage query fires on the Cx-first path — if the
    pre-dispatch checkpoint had accidentally short-circuited
    before `get_action_undo`, the test would surface a different
    "not found" message instead of "No undo metadata".

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * undo:: — 95 passed / 0 failed (tick 62: 93 -> tick 63: 95,
    +2 new parity tests across the two pre-dispatch branches)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (1m49s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
